text.title: OpenAI Chat Completions — Procedure
text.summary: 6-step procedure for shipping a Chat Completions call from spec to logged record. Each step has typed input + bounded action + observable output.

step.name: pick-model
step.input: Task class (extraction, classification, generation, reasoning), latency budget, cost ceiling.
step.action: Map to one of {gpt-4o-mini, gpt-4o, o3-mini, o1} using the routing table. Default to mini for inner loops.
step.decision-gate: If latency < 800ms required, skip o1; if reasoning chain > 5 hops, use o-series.

step.name: build-messages
step.input: System prompt + user content + (optional) prior assistant turns.
step.action: Construct `messages` list with explicit roles. Validate no role mixing inside a single message dict.

step.name: set-params
step.action: Set `temperature` (0.0–0.2 for extraction, 0.7+ for creative), `max_tokens` (sized to expected output × 1.5), `response_format` ({"type":"json_object"} when JSON), `seed` for reproducibility.

step.name: call-with-retry
step.action: Invoke via the tenacity-wrapped client (`templates/retry-client.py`). Retry only on `RateLimitError` and `APIError`. Fail-fast on `BadRequestError`.

step.name: check-finish-reason
step.action: If `length` → raise and retry with larger `max_tokens`. If `content_filter` → surface to operator. If `stop`/`tool_calls` → proceed.
step.decision-gate: Truncated content is never forwarded downstream.

step.name: log-usage
step.action: Persist a record matching the output contract (model, messages_hash, params, usage, finish_reason, content, latency_ms, request_id).
step.output: JSON record written to call log; cost accounted to the pipeline run.
